hawkish_phrases: financial conditions had tightened since the Governing Council's previous monetary policy meeting in early June
concerns were expressed about the risk of the exchange rate overshooting in the future
convincing evidence of a sustained convergence towards levels compatible with the ECB's inflation aim was still seen to be lacking
measures of underlying inflation remained low and had not yet shown convincing signs of a pick-up
the Governing Council needed to gain more policy space and flexibility to adjust policy
dovish_phrases: a very substantial degree of monetary accommodation was still needed for underlying inflation pressures to build up gradually
patience, persistence and prudence were needed
the current monetary policy stance remained appropriate
the Governing Council stood ready to increase the programme in terms of size and/or duration
key ECB interest rates to remain at their present levels for an extended period of time, and well past the horizon of the net asset purchases
summary: The Governing Council held all policy rates unchanged (MRO 0.00%, deposit -0.40%) and maintained net asset purchases at €60bn monthly until end-December 2017 or beyond, with unanimous agreement emphasizing that despite strengthening economic expansion, a very substantial degree of monetary accommodation was still needed as underlying inflation remained subdued and convergence to the inflation aim remained conditional on continued policy support.
